target.name: Etched
target.founder: Gavin Uberti, Chris Zhu, Robert Wachen

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $5.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $2.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Etched is a Series A-stage semiconductor company founded by Gavin Uberti, Chris Zhu, and Robert Wachen, currently valued at $5.0B post-money following $125M+ in total fundraising. The valuation assessment draws on a comp universe of 3 public semiconductor players (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private frontier-chip companies, with direct peer comparison to d-Matrix, Substrate, and Rain AI at the same stage.

Two quantitative methods apply to this valuation. Stage-progression anchors on Etched's current $5.0B post-money and applies a typical Series A-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.0x, implying $10.0B. Peer-median triangulation across three same-sector, same-stage comparables—d-Matrix at $2.0B, Substrate at $1.0B, and Rain AI at $500M—yields a median of $1.0B (range $500M–$2.0B). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 22.3x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied because Etched's revenue is not disclosed.

The triangulated range supporting Etched's valuation spans $850M (low) to $11.5B (high), with a median of $5.5B across the two applicable methods. The current $5.0B valuation sits near the lower-median bound. Key uncertainties center on Etched's actual revenue generation relative to public-market multiples—unavailable data that would materially sharpen the assessment—and the stability of private-comp valuations in a volatile frontier-semiconductor market.
